data.title: National Grid sees slight earnings impact from U.S. regulation, storms
data.description:
National Grid (NGG) down 1.8% in Monday's trading after saying it is performing in line with prior guidance but estimates a net impact of ~1 pence/share to underlying earnings due to refund charges related to a recent regulatory ruling and storm costs in the U.S.

The impact reflects the recognition of customer refund charges related to the March judgment from the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission on New England Transmission, as well as slightly higher than expected storm costs in its U.S. businesses, partly offset by slightly lower finance costs.

In November, National Grid (NGG) said growth in underlying EPS for the current financial year is expected in the 6%-8% range; in the financial year to March 2025, the utility reported underlying EPS of 73.3p.

data.title: Netflix wins sell-side praise for ad potential, content quality
data.description:
With the pursuit of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) in the rearview and a price hike successfully absorbed by subscribers, Netflix (NFLX) is now a lower-volatility business with a potential for “substantial” growth in its advertising business, leading Morgan Stanley and Wedbush to reiterate their bullish outlook on the stock with a corresponding hike to their respective price targets.

In a note to clients, Wedbush takes a closer look at Netflix’s (NFLX) advertising business and expects to see ad revenue to at least double to $3B this year, with “significant opportunities remaining in 2027 and beyond.”

“Netflix should continue to gain incrementally from its ad business by expanding partnerships, improving targeting, leveraging AI, and adding more live content,” notes Wedbush analyst Alicia Reese, with interactive ads enhancing its marketing capabilities.

Coupled with domestic subscription pricing, Reese remains positive on the company’s overall opportunity to expand revenue and reiterates her Outperform rating on Netflix (NFLX) with a 3% increase to her price target to $118.

Morgan Stanley’s Sean Diffley also sees additional ad revenue opportunities for Netflix (NFLX), while price hikes and an end to the Warner Bros. (WBD) pursuit also provide a tailwind to double-digit revenue growth.

Moreover, hours watched on Netflix are approaching 200B annually as quality of engagement is bolstered by live events and sporting events delivering outsized value and “must-see” moments.

“Nothing captures viewer attention more than live events, which carry urgency, unpredictability, and a sense of communal participation…the NFL is the pinnacle of this, so it is no surprise that Netflix is looking to expand its offering to 4 games from 2,” Diffley adds.

“Amidst concerns about the growth of absolute engagement, we see Netflix as the dominant player in the premium quality content space,” says Diffley, reiterating an Overweight rating on the stock with a 4.5% increase to his price target to $115.

Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha authors are also bullish on Netflix (NFLX) with a Buy consensus on the stock. Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating views the stock as a Hold with a score of 3.25 out of 5, assigning low marks to Netflix’s current valuation.

data.title: Large-cap IT stocks with A+ grade EPS revisions as earnings season begins
data.description:
As Q1 2026 earnings season gets underway, the Information Technology sector enters reporting season as one of the most closely watched, and highest-expectation, areas of the market.

This list ranks large-cap Information Technology stocks that have earned an A+ EPS revision grade from Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating system. The screening criteria include stocks within the IT sector with large market capitalizations and the highest possible EPS revision grade.

The list includes Ciena Corporation (CIEN), a communications equipment company with a market cap of $70.14 billion. Coherent Corp. (COHR) and Dell Technologies Inc. (DELL) follow, with Dell notably carrying a Strong Buy Quant rating of 4.94 alongside its A+ EPS revision grade.

The list features a diverse range of IT industries, including communications equipment, semiconductors, electronic manufacturing services, and application software. All 12 companies maintain the highest A+ EPS revision grade, with two stocks—Dell Technologies Inc. (DELL) and TTM Technologies, Inc. (TTMI)—also earning Strong Buy Quant ratings.

  1. Ciena Corporation (CIEN) EPS revision grade: A+

  2. Coherent Corp. (COHR) EPS revision grade: A+

  3. Dell Technologies Inc. (DELL) EPS revision grade: A+

  4. F5, Inc. (FFIV) EPS revision grade: A+

  5. Flex Ltd. (FLEX) EPS revision grade: A+

  6. HubSpot, Inc. (HUBS) EPS revision grade: A+

  7. MKS Inc. (MKSI) EPS revision grade: A+

  8. Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. (MPWR) EPS revision grade: A+

  9. Strategy Inc (MSTR) EPS revision grade: A+

  10. MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings, Inc. (MTSI) EPS revision grade: A+

  11. TD SYNNEX Corporation (SNX) EPS revision grade: A+

  12. TTM Technologies, Inc. (TTMI) EPS revision grade: A+

Tech ETFs: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT)

data.title: Amazon expands its auto business by adding mainstream brands
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) significantly expanded the Amazon Autos business by adding major new brands and rolling out to more cities. The expansion marks a substantial escalation of its bet on the automotive retail market. The e-commerce giant has quietly broadened its platform over the last 18 months, moving beyond its initial exclusive partnership with Hyundai (HYMLF) to now include vehicles from Jeep (STLA), Chevrolet (GM), Kia (HYMLF), Mazda (MZDAY), and Subaru (FUJHY) (FUJHF). 

Notably, the added auto brands transform Amazon Autos from a limited pilot into a comprehensive multi-brand marketplace, allowing customers to purchase brand-new cars from a much wider selection of manufacturers directly through the Amazon website. The growth strategy focuses on both inventory depth and geographic reach. While the service launched in late 2024 in 48 cities, the addition of mainstream U.S. and Asian brands significantly increases the addressable market for potential buyers. 

The Amazon Autos platform continues to operate on a marketplace model where local dealerships list their inventory, set haggle-free upfront prices, and maintain the customer relationship for final pickup and delivery, while Amazon facilitates the browsing, financing, and checkout process.

Looking ahead, the aggressive move positions Amazon (AMZN) to compete more directly with established online car retailers and traditional digital listing sites, leveraging its massive Prime member base and trusted checkout infrastructure to potentially capture a larger share of the car-buying market.

Related auto retail tickers: Carvana (CVNA), CarMax (KMX), Lithia Motors (LAD), AutoNation (AN), Asbury Automotive Group (ABG), CarGurus (CARG), Group 1 Automotive (GPI), Sonic Automotive (SAH), Penske Automotive Group (PAG), Vroom (VRM), Shift Technologies (SFTGQ), Rush Enterprise (RUSHA), Driven Brands (DRVN), Openlane (OPLN), ACV Auctions (ACVA), Cars.com (CARS), Tesla (TSLA), Toyota Motor (TM), Honda Motor (HMC), Nissan (NSANY), General Motors (GM), Rivian Automotive (RIVN), Lucid Group (LCID), Stellantis (STLA), and Ford Motor (F).

data.title: Adobe and Figma initiated with Neutral ratings from BTIG
data.description:
Adobe (ADBE) and Figma (FIG) both received Neutral ratings as BTIG initiated coverage on the digital content creation software firms.

Shares for both companies had edged up about 4% by noon trading on Monday.

"ADBE has long been the market-share leader for creative professionals through its Creative Cloud suite, helping ADBE drive ~$24B in FY25 revenue (+11% Y/Y) and delivering a margin profile few software companies may ever be able to match (FY25 GAAP OMs 37%)," said BTIG analysts, led by Nick Altmann, in an investor report. "That said, AI is raising a host of questions about the future of the creative ecosystem, fueling concerns about Adobe's longer-term revenue and margin trajectory. While we're encouraged by Adobe’s AI strategy and view current valuation levels as attractive (~11.5x CY27E GAAP Earnings vs. ~26x peers) and believe some of the worries are overblown, we seek greater conviction on how AI ultimately reshapes the creative space & ADBE's financial profile before recommending the shares."

Adobe's shares have declined 33% year to date.

Similarly, BTIG is impressed with Figma's design products but is waiting to see more of its AI monetization strategy in action.

"FIG's direct monetization efforts for its Make offering initiated more broadly in March (via the introduction of credit consumption and enforcement of credit limits) and early KPIs around usage (e.g., GM compression from significant inference costs, Make WAU's up 70% Q/Q, etc.) get us constructive on FIG's potential to be one of the earliest application software companies to monetize AI," Altmann noted. "That said, it remains difficult to decipher the degree to which monetization can happen in the NT and how much the initial usage will carry through once customers are charged."

data.title: Value is beating growth across the board this year
data.description:
Value stocks are beating growth stocks across every major U.S. market-cap category so far in 2026, signaling a notable shift in equity leadership away from the narrow growth trade that dominated much of the previous cycle, Oppenheimer Asset Management strategist John Stoltzfus wrote in a Monday note.

Through April 10, value has outpaced growth in the Russell 3000 (IWV), Russell 1000 (IWB), Russell Midcap (IWR), Russell 2000 (IWM) and Russell Microcap (IWC) indexes, according to Stoltzfus. The performance gap is most pronounced in smaller companies, where Russell 2000 Value (IWN) gained 9.7% compared to just 2.6% for growth (IWO). Russell Microcap Value climbed 11.2%, while its growth counterpart managed only 1.7%.

 

Among large caps, the Russell 1000 Value Index (VONV) has also topped the Russell 1000 Growth Index (VONG) since the start of the year, a meaningful development given the concentrated growth leadership that characterized much of last year, Stoltzfus wrote.

The shift marks a potential inflection point for investors who had grown accustomed to growth dominance.

"In our view, this represents a broadening in investor appetite for stock market capitalization segments and styles that underperformed in 2025 along with a desire for further diversification and away from over-concentrated positioning," the note said.

data.title: Peru says Southern Copper's Tia Maria mine under review, not canceled - report
data.description:
Peru's Mining Ministry said Southern Copper's (SCCO) Tia Maria copper project has not been canceled and that it is under review for verification that requirements have been met, RPP reported, citing an interview with Deputy Mining Minister Mayra Figueroa.

The project is not undergoing a technical or environmental review of any kind, Figueroa reportedly said in the interview.

Previous reports had said Peru's government revoked its authorization for the $1.8B project and sent it back to authorities for review, creating a new obstacle for the long-stalled project.

A legal review by Peru's Mining Council reportedly had declared null a resolution that had authorized the previous start of the Tia Maria operation.

"The file will be returned to the General Directorate of Mining, which will issue a new pronouncement in strict compliance with the guidelines established by the Mining Council and current regulations," the government had said.

Local farmers and residents have long feared the mine would cause pollution and threaten water supplies; the project, which aims to produce 120K metric tons/year of copper, is ~25% complete.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson Q1 preview: What to expect
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) is expected to report a 3.2% fall in its first-quarter earnings, due on April 14, before the opening bell.

The consensus EPS estimate is $2.68 on revenue of $23.61B, which represents a Y/Y jump of 7.8%. Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 7 upward revisions and 7 downward moves. Revenue estimates have seen 10 upward revisions.

This quarter, investors’ attention is expected to be around the loss of exclusivity (LOE) of the pharma giant’s super-blockbuster autoimmune drug Stelara. The drug is the second-largest revenue contributor for JNJ, after oncology drug Darzalex. It contributed to 6.5% of the firm’s total sales in 2025. Stelara sales were down 41.5% year-over-year.

Seeking Alpha analyst Dividend and Value Investor sees the company well-positioned, backed by drugs like Icotyde and Tremfya.

“In immunology, Icotyde, an oral treatment for plaque psoriasis, looks particularly promising. The peptide drug was approved as a first-line treatment only in mid-March this year. Also, I wouldn't underestimate Tremfya, which grew 40% in 2025, despite sales of already more than $5 billion,” the analyst noted.

For 2026, J&J projected operational sales growth in the range of 5.7% to 6.7% with a midpoint of $100 billion or 6.2%, while adjusted operational earnings per share are expected in the range of $11.28 to $11.48, translating to a growth of 5.5% at the midpoint.

2026 sales growth is expected to be driven by products including Tremfya, Darzalex, Carvykti, Erleada, Spravato, Rybrevant Plus Lazcluze and Caplyta in Innovative Medicine, and new launches in MedTech, the company added.

Over the last 2 years, JNJ has beaten EPS estimates 88% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 100% of the time, building investors’ expectations for another quarter of a solid beat. Year-to-date, the stock has advanced over 15%, giving attractive returns to investors.

data.title: RedCloud rallies 94% to $1.11 after signing $30M Saudi Arabia deal
data.description:
• RedCloud (RCT) announced on Monday that it had signed a five-year deal worth up to ~$30M to launch its RAID AI platform in Saudi Arabia’s ~$68B FMCG market.
  • The deal is expected to generate ~$6M annually, tied to RAID-generated revenues, aiming to reduce inefficiencies and improve how goods were bought, moved, and sold using real-time data.
  • This was its second major JV deal after the $50M Türkiye agreement, taking total contracted revenue to ~$80M and supporting its capital-light expansion strategy.
  • Investors reacted strongly to this deal, as RedCloud (RCT) is up 94.74% to $1.11 intraday, rising sharply from a previous close of $0.57.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CoreWeave climbs on recent deals; Macquarie upgrades stock to Outperform
data.description:
CoreWeave (CRWV) shares rallied 10% during Monday morning market action, and Macquarie upgraded the stock to Outperform from Neutral due to its recent deals with Meta (META) and Anthropic (ANTHRO).

On Friday, it was revealed Anthropic had agreed to lease data center capacity from CoreWeave to support rising demand for its artificial intelligence services, with the multi-year deal set to bring additional compute online starting later this year. This followed an agreement announced one day prior to provide Meta AI cloud capacity through December 2032, in a deal valued at about $21B.

Macquarie also increased its price target to $125 from $90.

"The recent headlines, in our view, validate CRWV's differentiated platform, unique access to hardware, and strategic positioning in AI ecosystems, which, when paired with a growing trend of compute diversification, may mean it is increasingly becoming structural to hyperscalers," said Macquarie analysts, led by Paul Golding, in an investor note.

"We still think recontracting and hyperscaler in-house capacity and accelerator progress once these initial scaled deals roll over will be important to watch in 3-5 years' time," he added. "But CRWV may be (even more) entrenched in the ecosystem (and deal timing more staggered) by then, and rolling back aggregate compute even as model efficiency improves could be seen as too risky in the competitive pursuit of AGI/ASI."

Other neocloud and AI data center companies were up as well on Monday. Nebius (NBIS) had surged 9%, IREN (IREN) had jumped 6%, while Applied Digital (APLD) and Hut 8 (HUT) had both climbed 5%.
